title: Whole Book Derivation Map
description: Fixed Opening → Boundary → Stations → Equal Step → Equal Cell → Sixfold Closure → Direction Families → Triangular Field → Geometry → Rightness/Ratio → Trigonometry → Coordinate Audit → Algebra

title: Four Branch Map
description: Triangular Field branching into Geometry, Trigonometry, Coordinate Geometry, and Algebra.

title: Compass Opening Fixed
description: Compass with two legs; label compass point, pencil point, fixed opening, unit reach. The diagram should be simple and uncluttered.

title: Same Opening After Station Transfer
description: The same compass opening shown at Station A and then at Station B. Use matching marks to show that the opening has not changed.

title: Center Station and Fixed Opening
description: Center O with compass point standing at O. Pencil point is one fixed opening away from O. Label the opening as the same reach that was fixed in Chapter 1.

title: Sweep Trace
description: Pencil sweeps around O and leaves a smooth boundary. Show the compass point still at O.

title: Boundary as Reached Places
description: Several points on the boundary, each joined back to O by the same reach. Use matching marks to show the reach is the same each time.

title: Selecting a Boundary Position
description: Show the boundary around center O. Mark one point on the boundary and label it A. Keep the diagram quiet: one center, one boundary, one chosen point.

title: Boundary Position Becomes Station
description: Show the compass point moved from O to A. Use the same opening mark as before so the reader sees that the opening has travelled unchanged.

title: Sweep from Boundary Station
description: Show original center O, original boundary, and boundary station A. Add a new sweep centered at A using the same opening.

title: Meeting with the Base Boundary
description: Show the sweep from A meeting the original boundary at B. Make the meeting point clear.

title: Equal-Opening Step A to B
description: Highlight segment AB. Use the same opening mark used for OA. The reader should see that AB is one use of the same opening.

title: Three Stations O, A, B
description: Show O at the center and A and B as adjacent boundary stations. Do not overlabel. The reader should first see the three points.

title: Three Equal-Opening Sides
description: Highlight OA, OB, and AB with the same tick mark or opening mark. The diagram should make 'three sides, one opening' obvious.

title: First Equal-Opening Cell
description: Lightly shade triangle OAB. Keep the equal-opening marks visible.

title: Boundary Step Sequence
description: Boundary with stations A0, A1, A2, A3, A4, and A5 appearing one step at a time. Show the same compass opening between neighboring stations.

title: Sixth Step Returns
description: The final step from A5 back to A0. Make the return visually clear.

title: Closed Six-Step Boundary Cycle
description: Completed six-station boundary cycle. Keep it plain. Do not overload it with later labels.

title: Six Central Cells
description: Six triangles around center O. Shade or outline the cells lightly.

title: Adjacent Cells Share a Radius
description: Two neighboring central cells sharing one center-to-boundary side.

title: Complete Turn Field
description: Full six-cell turn around O. The center should feel like the meeting place of the six cells.

title: Boundary Hexagon
description: Six boundary stations connected as a hexagonal outline. Keep the center faint or absent in the first version.

title: Hexagon with Six Radii
description: Same hexagon with center O joined to all six boundary stations. Show that the hexagon is carried by the six central cells.

title: Hexagon as Six Equal-Opening Cells
description: Hexagon decomposed into six central triangular cells.

title: Three Direction Pairs in the Hexagon
description: Highlight opposite side pairs of the hexagon in three visual styles. The point is simple: three pairs, three directions.

title: Direction Pairs in the Hexagon
description: A hexagon with opposite sides highlighted in matching styles. Show three pairs: one pair horizontal or nearly horizontal, and two slanted pairs. Keep the center and radii faint or optional.

title: Three Direction Families
description: The same hexagon with the three direction families extended lightly through the field. Use three line styles, not heavy color dependence.

title: Direction Family Without Coordinates
description: Same-direction recurrence shown without axes, numbers, or slope. The reader should see that direction is recognized by repeated way-of-running.

title: Extending One Direction Family
description: One pair of same-direction lines from the hexagon extended beyond the boundary. Keep everything else light.

title: Two Families Meet
description: Two extended direction families meeting outside the first hexagon, with new stations marked at their crossings.

title: Triangular Tessellation Emerges
description: Repeated triangular cells appearing beyond the first hexagon. Show that they share the same three directions.

title: First Field vs Extended Field
description: Original sixfold construction emphasized inside a larger triangular field. The reader should see that the large field grows from the first construction.

title: Local Relation in One Cell
description: One triangular cell with a side or relation marked. Include a small note showing why the relation is earned.

title: Lawful Transport to Another Cell
description: A second cell in the same field with the same source condition highlighted. Show the relation traveling by structure, not by appearance.

title: Invalid Copy by Appearance
description: A visually similar shape outside or unsupported by the field. Mark it gently as not licensed.

title: Triangle as Earned Cell
description: One triangular cell from the field. Keep the field faintly visible behind it. Mark the three source-earned sides.

title: Rhombus as Two Cells
description: Two adjacent triangular cells sharing one side. Shade the two cells together as one rhombus.

title: Hexagon as Six Cells
description: The central hexagon shown as six triangular cells around the center.

title: Shared Side Between Adjacent Cells
description: Two triangular cells sharing one side. Mark the shared side clearly.

title: Path Across Cells
description: A chain of triangular cells. Show a path passing from cell to cell through shared sides.

title: Boundary Path vs Interior Path
description: Show one path along the outside boundary and another path through interior shared sides.

title: One Cell as Area Seed
description: One triangular cell shaded as the first area piece.

title: Rhombus Equals Two Cells
description: A rhombus split into two triangular cells. Label it as two cells.

title: Hexagon Equals Six Cells
description: The central hexagon split into six triangular cells. Label it as six cells.

title: Larger Region by Cell Count
description: A larger region in the triangular field, shaded and counted by cells.

title: Opposite Boundary Connectors
description: Show the sixfold field with selected connectors between opposite boundary stations. Keep the diagram uncluttered. The reader should see the connectors before seeing any right-angle mark.

title: Selected Connectors Meeting at Center
description: Show two selected connectors meeting at the center or inside the field. Highlight the meeting point.

title: Right-Meeting Evidence
description: Add the right-angle marker only after the selected connectors are visible. The marker should feel like a label on earned structure, not an assumption.

title: Extracting a Right Triangle from the Field
description: Show the full field faintly, then highlight one right triangle inside it. The right meeting should be visible as one corner.

title: Side Roles
description: Show the same triangle with the side across from the right corner identified as the longest side. Do not overload with trigonometric labels yet.

title: Field Source Behind the Triangle
description: Show the triangle again with the field still visible behind it, so the reader sees that the triangle was extracted from the field.

title: 1, sqrt(3), 2 Triangle
description: Show one field-right triangle labeled with side packet 1, sqrt(3), and 2. Keep it embedded in or visibly linked to the field.

title: Same Triangle, Different Chosen Angle
description: Show the same triangle twice, choosing a different acute angle each time. Make clear that opposite and adjacent roles switch.

title: Ratio Packet Before Names
description: Show side comparisons as plain ratios before using sine, cosine, or tangent.

title: Sine as Opposite over Hypotenuse
description: Highlight the opposite side and hypotenuse relative to one chosen acute angle.

title: Cosine as Adjacent over Hypotenuse
description: Highlight the adjacent side and hypotenuse relative to the same chosen angle.

title: Tangent as Opposite over Adjacent
description: Highlight the opposite and adjacent sides relative to the same chosen angle.

title: Three Ratio Names on One Triangle
description: One summary triangle with the three named comparisons shown cleanly. Avoid clutter by using callouts if needed.

title: Ratio Family from One Triangle
description: Show sine, cosine, and tangent as three callouts from the same triangle. Make the shared sides visually obvious.

title: Complementary Side Role Exchange
description: Show the two acute angles in the same right triangle. Show how opposite and adjacent roles switch when the chosen angle changes.

title: Tangent as Ratio of Ratio Records
description: Show tangent connected to sine and cosine through shared side comparisons. Keep the diagram visual before symbolic.

title: Unit Circle as Organizer
description: Unit circle with center O, radius 1, and a chosen radius leaning from the center to the boundary. Keep the picture clean.

title: Projection Coordinates
description: From the end of the radius, drop a vertical line to the horizontal axis. Show horizontal reach and vertical reach.

title: Triangle Ratio to Circle Coordinate
description: Same right triangle shown first as a triangle, then placed inside the unit circle.

title: Unit Circle Overlaid on Source Field
description: Unit circle lightly overlaid on the constructed field. The circle organizes; it does not replace the field.

title: Constructed Stations Before Coordinates
description: A small triangular field with stations and cells but no axes or coordinate labels.

title: Coordinates Added Afterward
description: Same field with coordinate labels added after the construction is visible.

title: Coordinate Label as Memory Tag
description: One station shown with both its constructional role and its coordinate label.

title: Two Coordinate Records
description: Two stations in the field, each with a coordinate label.

title: Difference Vector
description: Arrow from the first station to the second station, labelled as the difference between the two coordinate records.

title: Norm as Distance Audit
description: Same arrow checked against a distance or norm rule. Keep the construction visible underneath.

title: Projection Components
description: Difference broken into field-direction components.

title: Direction Family Before Slope
description: One direction family highlighted in the triangular field, without axes.

title: Slope as Coordinate Record
description: Axes or coordinate labels added after the direction family is visible. Show slope as a record, not a source.

title: Line Equation as Direction Memory
description: Several stations on the same line with a simple equation label beside them.

title: From Constructed Relation to Symbol
description: A constructed side relation shown in a figure, then labeled with a symbol.

title: Symbol as Memory Token
description: The symbol shown beside the figure with an arrow back to the relation it remembers.

title: Formula as Compressed Diagram
description: A figure on one side and a short symbolic relation on the other. The formula should feel like a compressed record of the figure.

title: Missing Relation Marked as Unknown
description: A known structure with one side, distance, coordinate, or ratio marked x. The diagram should show that x belongs to an existing relation. It should not float by itself.

title: Equation as Constraint
description: The same structure beside an equation. Use arrows or matching labels to show which parts of the picture became symbols.

title: Solving Recovers the Missing Relation
description: The original structure after x has been found. Show the value returned to the place where the unknown stood.

title: Same Relation, Different Forms
description: One relation shown in several equivalent symbolic forms. Each form should be connected back to the same source relation.

title: Lawful vs Unlawful Transformation
description: Two-column diagram. Left column: a transformation that preserves equality. Right column: a move that changes only one side and breaks equality.

title: Final Branch Convergence
description: Four branches returning to one source-bound view. Geometry = constructed figure. Trigonometry = stable ratio. Coordinate geometry = audited position. Algebra = transformed relation.
